name: Status.io API v2
description:
Welcome to the official [Status.io](https://status.io) Developer API. This API is designed to help you seamlessly integrate your existing tools with your status page.

# API Testing

## Live Sandbox

Use the built-in API client to test and debug API calls, and view example requests in multiple programming languages.

**Caution:** These are live API calls and will make real changes to your status page. We strongly recommend creating a separate status page for testing purposes.

# Authentication

Include **x-api-id** and **x-api-key** in the header of every request.

API credentials are available in the API tab of your Dashboard.

**Note:** Each team member has their own unique API credentials.

# Error Handling

All API responses include a status object.

Check the **status.error** field ("yes" or "no") and refer to **status.message** for details.

## Success Example

```
    {"status":
        {
            "error": "no",
            "message": "OK"
        }
    }
```

## Failure Example

```
    {"status":
        {
            "error": "yes",
            "message": "Authentication failed"
        }
    }
```

## Invalid URLs

If an invalid URL or HTTP method is used, the API will respond with a **403 Forbidden** error.

## Validation Errors

If input parameters are of the wrong type or missing, the API will return a **400 Bad Request** error.

# Client Libraries

We provide official client libraries in several languages to help you get started quickly:

Go - [statusio-go](https://github.com/statusio/statusio-go)

Node.js - [statusio-node](https://github.com/statusio/statusio-node)

PHP - [statusio-php](https://github.com/statusio/statusio-php)

Python - [statusio-python](https://github.com/statusio/statusio-python)

Ruby - [statusio-ruby](https://github.com/statusio/statusio-ruby)

Rust - [statusio unofficial](https://github.com/SecurityInsanity/statusio)

*Created and maintained by a third-party developer.*

## Community Development

If you have built a library or plugin that integrates with the Status.io API, we welcome community contributions and would be pleased to feature your work. Please reach out to share your integration.

# Data Format

All requests and responses use JSON.

Include the following header in all requests:

```
Content-Type: application/json
```

## Dates and Times

All timestamps returned by the API are in UTC.

However, custom time zone settings are automatically applied for viewers of your status page.

# Best Practices

## Use Caching

Whenever possible, cache API responses server-side.

Avoid making a new API call for every page hit, especially under load, as this can cause your connection to be throttled.

If the data doesn’t change frequently, caching will improve performance and reliability.

## Use Reasonable Polling Intervals

Avoid checking for updates too frequently. For example, polling every second for a status update offers no real benefit—the highest meaningful resolution is one minute. Requests more frequent than this may be rate-limited or blocked.

## Only Send Meaningful Updates

Send updates only when status changes occur. Avoid repeatedly reporting the same status, such as continuously reporting that a component is operational when no change has occurred.

## Limit Concurrent Requests

Only send **one API request at a time**. If you need to send multiple updates, queue them and send them sequentially, waiting at least **1 second between requests** to avoid triggering rate limits.

## Expect Network Issues

Network connectivity can be intermittent. Design your integration to handle timeouts and failures gracefully. Always implement proper error handling and retry logic—never assume every request will succeed.

# Support

We are here to help. If you encounter any issues or have suggestions for improvement, please reach out: hello@status.io

# Security

We support and appreciate responsible disclosure.

To report a security issue, contact our security team at: security@status.io.

If you’re sharing sensitive information, please encrypt it using our [PGP key](https://status.io/public_key.txt).
